---
title: "Melanogenesis Constraints — Project Overview"
subtitle: "PEQG 2026 Poster Analysis"
---

## About this project

This site documents the analysis pipeline for the PEQG 2026 poster:
**"Testing Network-Based Predictions of Genetic Constraint in Melanogenesis."**

We test whether a gene's position in the melanogenesis signaling network predicts
its evolutionary constraint — combining LoF intolerance (LOEUF), phylogenetic
conservation (PhyloP), tissue expression breadth (GTEx), and population genomics
(nucleotide diversity π, PBS) across 5 populations.

**Gene set:** Raghunath et al. 2015 — 129-gene melanocyte signaling network,
classified into 6 functional categories.

---

## Analysis phases

| Phase | Analysis | Status |
|---|---|---|
| [Phase 0](phase0.qmd) | Network position (betweenness centrality) + KEGG pathway connectivity vs. LOEUF | ✓ Complete |
| [Phase 1](phase1.qmd) | PhyloP conservation + GTEx tissue expression breadth vs. LOEUF | ✓ Complete |
| [Phase 1.3](phase1_ancestry.qmd) | gnomAD v4 ancestry-stratified LOEUF (NFE, AFR, EAS, SAS, ASJ) | ✓ Complete |
| [Phase 2](phase2.qmd) | Population genomics: π and PBS across African, Melanesian, South Asian, East Asian, European | ✓ Complete |

---

## Key findings

- **Pigment-specific genes are significantly more LoF-tolerant** than generic signaling genes
  (Kruskal-Wallis p = 1.70×10⁻⁸), consistent with the recessive biology hypothesis —
  heterozygous LoF carries no fitness penalty.
- The tolerance ordering is **preserved across ancestry groups** (NFE/AFR LOEUF concordant),
  ruling out European ascertainment bias as the sole driver.
- **Tissue expression breadth strongly predicts constraint** (Spearman ρ > 0, p < 0.001):
  broadly expressed genes are more LoF-intolerant regardless of pigmentation function.
- **MC1R paradox:** highest LOEUF (1.967) in the network yet expressed in 53/54 tissues;
  African PBS elevated (84–87th percentile within network) despite high π — consistent
  with African purifying selection acting on *missense* variants, invisible to LoF metrics.

---

## Phase 0 — LOEUF constraint analysis

These analyses are complete and ready for the poster.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
from scipy import stats
import scikit_posthocs as sp
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

loeuf = pd.read_excel('data/LOEUF_by_functional_category.xlsx',
                      sheet_name='All Genes by Category')
loeuf.columns = ['gene', 'functional_category', 'disease_class',
                  'LOEUF', 'pLI', 'betweenness_centrality']

CATEGORY_ORDER = [
    'Pigment-specific', 'Developmental/NC', 'Generic signaling',
    'Cytokines/growth factors', 'Apoptosis/cell death', 'Other',
]
CATEGORY_COLORS = {
    'Pigment-specific':         '#D94040',
    'Developmental/NC':         '#E8907E',
    'Generic signaling':        '#F5C242',
    'Cytokines/growth factors': '#4878CF',
    'Apoptosis/cell death':     '#6BAD6B',
    'Other':                    '#B0B0B0',
}
CATEGORY_SHORT = {
    'Pigment-specific':         'Pigment-\nspecific',
    'Developmental/NC':         'Developmental\n/NC',
    'Generic signaling':        'Generic\nsignaling',
    'Cytokines/growth factors': 'Cytokines/\nGF',
    'Apoptosis/cell death':     'Apoptosis/\ncell death',
    'Other':                    'Other',
}
```

### LOEUF by functional category

Genes in the Raghunath network classified into six functional categories.
Kruskal-Wallis test with Dunn's posthoc (Bonferroni correction).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
df_clean = loeuf.dropna(subset=['LOEUF'])
groups = [df_clean.loc[df_clean['functional_category'] == c, 'LOEUF'].values
          for c in CATEGORY_ORDER]
kw_stat, kw_p = stats.kruskal(*[g for g in groups if len(g) > 0])
dunn = sp.posthoc_dunn(df_clean, val_col='LOEUF',
                        group_col='functional_category', p_adjust='bonferroni')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 7))
bp_data = [df_clean.loc[df_clean['functional_category'] == c, 'LOEUF'].values
           for c in CATEGORY_ORDER]
ns = [len(v) for v in bp_data]
positions = list(range(len(CATEGORY_ORDER)))

bp = ax.boxplot(bp_data, positions=positions, widths=0.6,
                patch_artist=True, showfliers=False,
                medianprops=dict(color='black', linewidth=1.5),
                whiskerprops=dict(color='gray'), capprops=dict(color='gray'))
for patch, cat in zip(bp['boxes'], CATEGORY_ORDER):
    patch.set_facecolor(CATEGORY_COLORS[cat])
    patch.set_alpha(0.85)
    patch.set_edgecolor('gray')

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
for i, (vals, cat) in enumerate(zip(bp_data, CATEGORY_ORDER)):
    jitter = rng.uniform(-0.15, 0.15, len(vals))
    ax.scatter(np.full(len(vals), i) + jitter, vals,
               c=CATEGORY_COLORS[cat], s=20, alpha=0.7,
               edgecolors='white', linewidths=0.3, zorder=3)

# Significance brackets
sig_pairs = []
for i in range(len(CATEGORY_ORDER)):
    for j in range(i + 1, len(CATEGORY_ORDER)):
        c1, c2 = CATEGORY_ORDER[i], CATEGORY_ORDER[j]
        if c1 in dunn.index and c2 in dunn.columns:
            p = dunn.loc[c1, c2]
            if p < 0.05:
                sig_pairs.append((i, j, p))
sig_pairs.sort(key=lambda x: x[1] - x[0])

y_max = max(v for vals in bp_data for v in vals)
bracket_start = y_max + 0.12
bracket_step = 0.14
for k, (i, j, p) in enumerate(sig_pairs):
    y = bracket_start + k * bracket_step
    p_text = '***' if p < 0.001 else '**' if p < 0.01 else '*'
    ax.plot([i, i, j, j], [y - 0.03, y, y, y - 0.03], color='black', lw=1)
    ax.text((i + j) / 2, y + 0.01, p_text, ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=13)

top_y = bracket_start + len(sig_pairs) * bracket_step + 0.05
# Set ylim before placing n= labels and KW box
ax.set_ylim(-0.15, top_y + 0.18)

for i, n in enumerate(ns):
    ax.text(i, -0.1, f'n={n}', ha='center', fontsize=10,
            color='#555555', fontstyle='italic')

ax.text(len(CATEGORY_ORDER) / 2 - 0.5, top_y + 0.02,
        f'Kruskal-Wallis p = {kw_p:.2e}', fontsize=12, ha='center', va='bottom',
        bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='white', edgecolor='gray', alpha=0.9))

ax.set_xticks(positions)
ax.set_xticklabels([CATEGORY_SHORT[c] for c in CATEGORY_ORDER],
                   fontsize=12, rotation=30, ha='right')
ax.set_ylabel('LOEUF (lower = more constrained)', fontsize=13)
ax.set_title('LoF intolerance by functional category', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', loc='left')
fig.subplots_adjust(bottom=0.18, top=0.92)
plt.show()
```

### LOEUF by disease class

Genes classified as syndromic/pleiotropic vs. isolated pigmentation phenotype.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
disease_map = {
    'Syndromic/developmental': 'Syndromic /\ndevelopmental',
    'Isolated pigment':        'Isolated\npigment',
    'Other Mendelian':         'Other\nMendelian',
    'Not classified':          'Not\nclassified',
}
disease_order = list(disease_map.keys())
disease_colors = {
    'Syndromic/developmental': '#6B5FCF',
    'Isolated pigment':        '#D94040',
    'Other Mendelian':         '#4878CF',
    'Not classified':          '#B0B0B0',
}

df_d = loeuf.dropna(subset=['LOEUF'])
bp_data = [df_d.loc[df_d['disease_class'] == c, 'LOEUF'].values
           for c in disease_order]
ns = [len(v) for v in bp_data]

# Mann-Whitney: syndromic vs. isolated pigment
synd = df_d.loc[df_d['disease_class'] == 'Syndromic/developmental', 'LOEUF']
isol = df_d.loc[df_d['disease_class'] == 'Isolated pigment', 'LOEUF']
u_stat, mw_p = stats.mannwhitneyu(synd, isol, alternative='two-sided')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
positions = list(range(len(disease_order)))
bp = ax.boxplot(bp_data, positions=positions, widths=0.5,
                patch_artist=True, showfliers=False,
                medianprops=dict(color='black', linewidth=1.5),
                whiskerprops=dict(color='gray'), capprops=dict(color='gray'))
for patch, cat in zip(bp['boxes'], disease_order):
    patch.set_facecolor(disease_colors[cat])
    patch.set_alpha(0.85)
    patch.set_edgecolor('gray')

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
for i, (vals, cat) in enumerate(zip(bp_data, disease_order)):
    if len(vals) == 0:
        continue
    jitter = rng.uniform(-0.12, 0.12, len(vals))
    ax.scatter(np.full(len(vals), i) + jitter, vals,
               c=disease_colors[cat], s=20, alpha=0.7,
               edgecolors='white', linewidths=0.3, zorder=3)

y_max = max(v for vals in bp_data for v in vals if len(vals) > 0)
ax.set_ylim(-0.15, y_max + 0.45)

# n= labels at fixed position inside plot
for i, n in enumerate(ns):
    ax.text(i, -0.1, f'n={n}', ha='center', fontsize=10,
            color='#555555', fontstyle='italic')

# Significance bracket
bh = y_max + 0.12
ax.plot([0, 0, 1, 1], [bh - 0.03, bh, bh, bh - 0.03], color='black', lw=1)
ax.text(0.5, bh + 0.01, f'p = {mw_p:.2e}', ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=12)

ax.set_xticks(positions)
ax.set_xticklabels([disease_map[c] for c in disease_order], fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('LOEUF (lower = more constrained)', fontsize=13)
ax.set_title('LoF intolerance by disease class', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', loc='left')
fig.subplots_adjust(bottom=0.12, top=0.92)
plt.show()
```

**Key result:** Complete separation between disease classes — syndromic/developmental
genes are universally constrained (low LOEUF), while isolated pigmentation genes
are LoF-tolerant (high LOEUF), p = `{python} f"{mw_p:.2e}"`.

### Summary statistics

```{python}
#| echo: false
summary = (df_clean.groupby('functional_category')['LOEUF']
           .agg(['count', 'median', 'mean'])
           .round(3)
           .rename(columns={'count': 'N', 'median': 'Median LOEUF', 'mean': 'Mean LOEUF'}))
summary = summary.loc[[c for c in CATEGORY_ORDER if c in summary.index]]
summary
```
